name: toggle-disabled
visual_summary: A toggle button with a disabled state, used to indicate an action is unavailable or unable to be performed. Typically used for situations where an action requires a user action to become available, making it accessible and usable again. This component can be a crucial element in UX/UI design to provide a clear and intuitive user experience.
generation_prompt:
Create a production-ready button component with the following characteristics:

Name: toggle-disabled
Description: A toggle button with a disabled state, used to indicate an action is unavailable or unable to be performed. Typically used for situations where an action requires a user action to become available, making it accessible and usable again. This component can be a crucial element in UX/UI design to provide a clear and intuitive user experience.
Style: light, flat, standard
Interactions: clickable, tappable, disabled
Tags: toggle, button, disabled, stateful, ux

Requirements:
- Works in React with TypeScript by default but adaptable to Vue, Svelte, or plain HTML/CSS
- Uses Tailwind CSS for styling
- Fully accessible with ARIA labels and keyboard navigation
- Responsive and mobile-friendly
- No external dependencies beyond React and Tailwind unless necessary
- Include all necessary imports
- Export as default with sensible prop types

Generate clean production-ready code only. No explanations.
